Page Search ads are going to show in the Google search results page, either at the top or the bottom of the page, or they're going to show up on Google search partner websites like AOL or ask.com. These ads are triggered in response to user search engine queries, and your ads are going to be seen by people searching for the keywords you select. This allows you to isolate and target hot, warm, and cold traffic audiences through your Page Search ads. And then finally, what is the ad format? The ad format is going to be 30-character text headlines plus 90-character text descriptions. You can have up to 15 headlines and up to four text descriptions. You don't have to use all of them, but we definitely recommend using multiple. And as you find more winning headlines, you can build that out over time. And you can also use ad extensions. So next up here, let's talk about search keyword intent. So when you're, as we discussed, your ads are only going to be served to people for the keywords that you select. So it's really important to have an understanding of intent and search keywords. So here we have a chart to explain this. And we've outlined four different tiers of intent. The first tier is the brand tier, which I just showed you. The second tier is the product aware tier. These are people that know your product, but they're not sure if it's right for them. Then tier two is the solution aware tier. I guess this is the third tier, solution aware. These are people who are looking for types of solutions. They know that solutions exist. They're not sure which solution they want to use. And then finally have people that are problem aware. They don't know that solutions exist. And then you can see keyword examples here, right? Under the brand tier, these are going to be people going, searching Rayban, Rayban Wayfair, Rayban coupon code. They're very specifically looking for your brand or your product name. Show them that you are the official brand that they're looking for and give them an offer that's going to make them want to act. If you're in tier one product aware, people are going to be searching things that help them figure out if your product is for them. So they might write a comparison like Rayban versus Maui Gym. They may say, are Raybans worth it? They might say Raybans for small faces. They want to know if the glasses are going to fit them. They might look up Rayban reviews. ## [09:07 - 12:09]
